top_signals.title: Russians covertly trained by China return to fight in Ukraine, sources say
top_signals.summary: While China and Russia have held several joint military exercises since the invasion of Ukraine, Beijing has repeatedly said it is neutral and presents itself as a peace mediator.
top_signals.why_it_matters: Geopolitical escalation affecting commodities, shipping and cross-asset volatility.

top_signals.title: 'Massive outperformance': Goldman Sachs on why North Asian stocks are beating those in the south - CNBC
top_signals.summary: 'Massive outperformance': Goldman Sachs on why North Asian stocks are beating those in the south  CNBC
top_signals.why_it_matters: Commodity repricing impacting inflation expectations and industrial supply chains.

top_signals.title: Utilities Are Betting Billions on a Technology That Could Become Obsolete
top_signals.summary: If we think of electricity purely as a commodity, then we should apply to it the one financial rule that dominates all commodity markets everywhere: the lowest cost producer always wins. The logic behind this is simple. Why would anyone pay more for an identical product, scarcity concerns notwithstanding? If a pound (or kilo) of say sugar was $5 at store A and $10 at store B, it’s no mystery whose inventory will sell out first. The same logic applies to the production of electricity. And, to us, that’s why industry leaders have been…
